Empires & Puzzles Max Review: The Mighty Pets Healer Guide

Max, the artist formerly known as Toto, brings massive heals and even bigger minions to the battlefield. Is this slow-speed Schnauzer worth your tonics in today's fast-paced meta? We break down the stats, the strategy, and the drama behind the name change.
Max Hero Review: Why This Dog's Bark Is As Big As His Bite
Max, The Storytelling Shepherd: Big Heals, Beefy Puppies, and a New Name
Max, a 5-star Nature hero from the

family, is a bit of a celebrity—mostly because he had to change his name to avoid a lawsuit from a certain Wizard. Originally introduced as "Toto" in late 2022, this slow-speed Druid is a classic support tank designed to keep your team alive through sheer volume of health and minions. While he lacks the flashy speed of modern heroes, he compensates with some of the beefiest minions in the game.
This review focuses on the base version of Max.

Max is depicted as a wise, perhaps slightly grumpy, Schnauzer-esque canine standing on two legs. Dressed in druidic robes adorned with leaves and carrying a wooden staff that sprouts yellow flowers, he looks like he's about to lecture you on the importance of recycling. The art style fits the whimsical yet slightly odd aesthetic of the Mighty Pets family perfectly. It’s a definite upgrade from "just a dog in a basket," giving him a character that feels like a protector of the forest—or at least the protector of his own backyard.

Looking at the raw numbers, Max shows his age. With a base power of around 875 (and significantly lower stats than the 1300+ behemoths released in 2025/2026), he is statistically behind the curve. His defense and health were considered tank-worthy in 2022, but today they are merely "decent." However, because his primary value comes from percentage-based healing and minion stats, he scales surprisingly well with Limit Breaks and Emblems. You
to invest heavily in his stats to make his minions survive modern AoE hits.
"Puppy Tales" is a slow skill that tries to do three things at once, and it succeeds wildly at two of them.
This is a substantial chunk of health, enough to bring a dying team back into the fight.
This is the main event. A minion with
is effectively a hefty meat shield that bites back hard. Most minions hover around 10-20% HP; Max's puppies are practically half a hero.

(Fire) is incredibly niche. It’s a mechanic designed to punish mono-red attackers or red snipers, but against a rainbow defense or non-red teams, it does literally nothing. It's a nice bonus when it works, but don't build your strategy around it.
Let's break down why those puppies are scary.
Slow (12 tiles). With a lvl 29 Magic/Styx troop and his 5% passive mana boost, you can shave this down, but he will rarely keep pace with Very Fast heroes.
If you get Max to ~1800 HP and ~1000 Attack (LB1 + Emblems):
~630 HP (This is higher than some 3-star heroes!)

Between the direct heal and the minion meat shield, Max effectively gives each ally ~1400+ HP worth of survivability. That is massive.

The Mighty Pets family brings some very interesting mechanics that reward minion play.

Upon release, "Toto" was seen as a solid but slow healer who suffered from comparison to Cleopatra. He was good, but not "meta-breaking" outside of Minion Wars.

Max has been heavily power-crept in terms of raw stats and speed. Modern healers often cleanse, boost health, or buff attack at Average speed. Max's Slow speed is a major liability in an era of 6-tile kills.
Max is not a top-tier defense hero anymore. However, he is a
superstar and a PvE monster. In modes where speed is equalized or irrelevant (Towers, Map Stages), his massive sustain is still top-class.
Max needs to survive. His entire kit revolves on him casting that one big spell to reset the board state.

Max shouldn't be your only healer in a standard raid, but he can be the anchor of a "Minion Spam" team.

If you pulled Max early, congratulations! He will carry you through World 1 and 2 easily. His minions are stronger than your other heroes. Level him up, but be aware he might feel "slow" in raids.

Max is a luxury toy. If you have the mats and love Minion Wars or Rush, he's worth it. If you are looking for a primary war healer, there are better, faster options. Do not LB2 him unless you use him in your primary Rush Defense.
The community has a love-hate relationship with this dog.
Players were baffled when "Toto" became "Max." One player joked,
"Sir Woofenstein would have been a better name... Max... lame."
From day one, the consensus was
Many compared him unfavorably to Cleopatra.
Players realized early on that his -80% damage reduction was
"A bit too niche... The perfect counter to Khufu, but useless otherwise."

Max is a hero out of time. His minions are legendary, but his speed is a relic. He is the goodest boy who tries his best, and when he works, he is spectacular—filling the screen with puppies that bite like wolves. But in a world of hypersonic ninjas and goblins, Max is often still putting on his shoes when the race is already over.
A situational king for Rush and Minion lovers, but a bench-warmer for the general meta.
